title: Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao

seo.imageCaption: Enjoy the iconic crispy, sweet topping and soft, fluffy bread of homemade Pineapple Buns (Bo Lo Bao) – a classic Chinese bakery treat!

description: Pineapple Buns, or Bo Lo Bao, are a beloved classic from Hong Kong bakeries, famed for their signature crispy, sweet crust resembling a pineapple's texture, hence the name. Beneath this delightful topping lies a soft, fluffy milk bread that is simply irresistible. This recipe brings the authentic taste of this iconic treat right into your home kitchen.

ingredients.notes: egg yolk mixed with 1 tsp water

instructions.title: Prepare the Bread Dough
instructions.text: In a large mixing bowl, combine the bread flour, sugar, yeast, and salt. Add the beaten egg and warm milk. Mix with a spatula until a shaggy dough forms. Turn the dough out onto a clean surface and knead for 5-7 minutes until it comes together. Add the softened butter and continue kneading for another 8-10 minutes until the dough is smooth, elastic, and passes the windowpane test.

instructions.title: First Proofing
instructions.text: Place the dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover with plastic wrap, and let it proof in a warm place for 60-90 minutes, or until it has doubled in size.

instructions.title: Make the Pineapple Topping
instructions.text: While the dough proofs, prepare the topping. In a separate bowl, cream together the cold cubed butter and sugar until light and fluffy. Beat in the egg yolk. Sift in the all-purpose flour, milk powder if using, and baking powder. Mix until just combined to form a soft, cookie-like dough. Cover and refrigerate until ready to use.

instructions.title: Shape the Dough and Topping
instructions.text: Once the bread dough has doubled, punch it down gently to release air. Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces (about 50-55g each). Roll each piece into a smooth ball. Divide the chilled pineapple topping into 8 equal portions. Flatten each topping portion into a disc large enough to cover about two-thirds of a bread dough ball.

instructions.title: Assemble the Buns and Second Proofing
instructions.text: Carefully place a topping disc over each bread dough ball, pressing gently to adhere and mold it around the top. Use a knife or dough scraper to lightly score a crisscross pattern on the topping, mimicking a pineapple. Place the assembled buns on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving space between them. Cover loosely with plastic wrap and let them proof again for 30-45 minutes, or until visibly puffy.

instructions.title: Bake the Pineapple Buns
instructions.text: Preheat your oven to 180 degrees Celsius (350F). Just before baking, gently brush the egg wash over the scored topping of each bun, avoiding the exposed bread. Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until the topping is golden brown and crispy, and the bread is cooked through. The internal temperature of the bread should reach 90-95 degrees Celsius (195-205F).

instructions.title: Cool and Serve
instructions.text: Remove the pineapple buns from the oven and transfer them to a wire rack to cool slightly. Serve warm for the best experience, perhaps with a slice of butter tucked inside, a classic Hong Kong style.

tips: For an extra fluffy bread dough, ensure your milk is lukewarm (around 38-40C) to activate the yeast properly. Avoid milk that is too hot, which can kill the yeast.
To achieve a perfectly crispy topping, make sure your butter for the topping is cold and cut into small cubes. This helps create a flaky, scone-like texture.

Pineapple buns are best enjoyed fresh and warm. If storing, keep them in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 days. Reheat gently in an oven or toaster oven to revive the crispy topping.
article:
Unlocking the Secret to Perfect Pineapple Buns (Bo Lo Bao) at Home

There’s a special kind of magic in biting into a freshly baked Pineapple Bun, or Bo Lo Bao. The soft, sweet bread underneath, crowned with a crisp, crumbly, sugary crust, offers a delightful contrast of textures and flavors that has captivated hearts in Hong Kong, Macau, and beyond. Often enjoyed with a cup of milk tea or coffee, these iconic pastries are a staple in Chinese bakeries and cha chaan tengs (Hong Kong-style diners). If you've ever dreamt of recreating that iconic flavor at home, this comprehensive Pineapple Bun Recipe is your guide to achieving bakery-quality results in your own kitchen.

## What is Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao?

Despite its name, the Pineapple Bun (Bo Lo Bao in Cantonese) contains no actual pineapple. The name comes from the distinctive criss-cross pattern on its golden-brown topping, which resembles the skin of a pineapple. Originating in Hong Kong, this beloved bun is a testament to the region's unique culinary fusion. The essence of a perfect Bo Lo Bao lies in its duality: a tender, slightly sweet yeast bread forms the base, while a sugary, buttery cookie-like crust shatters delicately with each bite. This topping, made from flour, sugar, butter, and egg, is applied just before baking, creating that signature "pineapple" texture and irresistible crunch. While the traditional Pineapple Bun Recipe focuses on the classic, understanding its roots enhances the baking experience. It's more than just a pastry; it's a cultural icon, a comforting snack, and a testament to the simple joys of Chinese baking.

## Ingredients You Need

Mastering this Pineapple Bun Recipe starts with gathering the right ingredients. Using fresh, quality components will make a significant difference in the final taste and texture of your buns.

**For the Dough:**
*   300g Bread Flour (or All-Purpose Flour for a softer crumb)
*   50g Granulated Sugar
*   3g Instant Yeast
*   3g Salt
*   1 Large Egg (about 50g), at room temperature
*   120ml Warm Milk (around 35-40°C / 95-105°F)
*   40g Unsalted Butter, softened

**For the Pineapple Topping:**
*   60g Unsalted Butter, softened
*   60g Granulated Sugar
*   1 Large Egg Yolk (about 18g)
*   100g All-Purpose Flour
*   15g Milk Powder (optional, but enhances flavor and color)
*   1 tbsp Milk (if topping is too stiff)

**For Egg Wash:**
*   1 Egg, beaten (for brushing the dough before applying topping)
*   1 Egg Yolk, beaten with 1 tbsp water (for brushing the topping)

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Following this detailed Pineapple Bun Recipe will ensure your buns come out perfectly, from the soft interior to the crisp, golden topping.

**Part 1: Making the Dough**

1.  **Activate Yeast:** In a large bowl, combine the warm milk, 1 teaspoon of the sugar, and the instant yeast. Stir gently and let it sit for 5-10 minutes until foamy, indicating the yeast is active.
2.  **Combine Dry Ingredients:** In a separate bowl, whisk together the bread flour, remaining sugar, and salt.
3.  **Mix Dough:** Add the beaten egg to the yeast mixture. Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients. Mix with a spatula until a shaggy dough forms.
4.  **Knead:** Transfer the dough to a lightly floured surface or a stand mixer with a dough hook. Knead for 8-10 minutes until the dough is smooth and elastic.
5.  **Add Butter:** Gradually add the softened butter, kneading until fully incorporated and the dough is shiny and passes the windowpane test (you can stretch a small piece thin enough to see light through it without tearing).
6.  **First Proof:** Place the dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover with plastic wrap, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 to 1.5 hours, or until doubled in size.
7.  **Divide and Shape:** Gently punch down the dough to release air. Divide it into 8-10 equal pieces (around 60-70g each). Roll each piece into a smooth ball. Place them on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving space between each.
8.  **Second Proof:** Cover the shaped buns loosely with plastic wrap or a damp cloth and let them rise again for 30-45 minutes, or until visibly puffy.

**Part 2: Making the Pineapple Topping**

1.  **Cream Butter and Sugar:** In a medium bowl, cream the softened butter and granulated sugar together until light and fluffy.
2.  **Add Egg Yolk:** Beat in the egg yolk until well combined.
3.  **Add Dry Ingredients:** Sift in the all-purpose flour and milk powder (if using). Mix until a soft, uniform dough forms. If the mixture is too dry, add 1 tbsp of milk gradually.
4.  **Chill:** Form the topping dough into a log, wrap it in plastic wrap, and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to firm up. This makes it easier to handle.

**Part 3: Assembly and Baking**

1.  **Preheat Oven:** Preheat your oven to 180°C (350°F).
2.  **Prepare Topping Discs:** Take the chilled topping dough. Divide it into the same number of pieces as your bread dough balls. Flatten each piece between two sheets of parchment paper or plastic wrap into a thin disc, slightly larger than your proofed bread buns.
3.  **Apply Topping:** Lightly brush the tops of the proofed bread buns with the beaten whole egg (egg wash). Carefully peel off one side of the parchment paper from a topping disc and invert it onto a bread bun. Gently press the topping onto the bun, shaping it to cover the top and slightly down the sides.
4.  **Score Topping:** Using a sharp knife or a dough scraper, gently score a criss-cross pattern onto the topping of each bun. Be careful not to cut too deep into the bread itself.
5.  **Final Egg Wash:** Brush the scored topping with the egg yolk wash (egg yolk mixed with water). This gives the buns their beautiful golden color.
6.  **Bake:** Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until the topping is golden brown and the buns are cooked through.
7.  **Cool:** Transfer the baked Pineapple Buns to a wire rack to cool slightly before serving.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

Achieving the perfect texture and flavor in your homemade Bo Lo Bao can be made easier with these expert tips. These will elevate your homemade Pineapple Bun Recipe from good to absolutely phenomenal.

*   **Accurate Measurements:** Baking is a science. Use a kitchen scale for precise measurements, especially for flour, to ensure the correct dough consistency.
*   **Don't Over-Knead or Under-Knead:** Proper kneading develops gluten, which is crucial for a soft, chewy bread. Over-kneading can make the dough tough, while under-kneading results in a dense crumb. Aim for the windowpane test.
*   **Proofing Environment:** A warm, draft-free environment is essential for proper yeast activation and rising. You can create one by placing the dough in a slightly warm oven (turned off!) with a bowl of hot water.
*   **Chill the Topping Well:** A firm topping dough is much easier to handle and roll out without tearing. Don't skip the chilling step.
*   **Handle Topping Gently:** The topping is delicate. When applying it, try to handle it minimally to prevent it from cracking before baking.
*   **Scoring:** Score the topping gently but decisively. A sharp, thin knife works best. The scores expand during baking, creating the signature pineapple pattern.
*   **Even Baking:** Rotate your baking sheet halfway through baking if your oven has hot spots, ensuring all buns bake evenly.
*   **Bread Flour vs. All-Purpose:** Bread flour contains more protein, leading to a chewier bun. All-purpose flour will yield a softer, more cake-like crumb. Experiment to find your preference.

## Serving Suggestions

Pineapple Buns are incredibly versatile and can be enjoyed in various ways.

*   **Classic "Bo Lo Yau":** Slice the bun horizontally and insert a thick slab of cold, unsalted butter. The contrast between the warm bun and cold butter is heavenly.
*   **With Milk Tea or Coffee:** This is the quintessential Hong Kong breakfast or afternoon tea pairing.
*   **Plain:** Enjoy them as they are, fresh from the oven, appreciating the simple flavors and textures.
*   **As a Snack:** Perfect for a mid-morning or afternoon treat.
*   **Dessert:** Serve alongside fresh fruit or a scoop of ice cream for a delightful dessert experience.

## Variations and Substitutions

Once you've mastered the basic Pineapple Bun Recipe, don't hesitate to experiment with these exciting variations to customize your Bo Lo Bao.

*   **Fillings:**
    *   **Custard:** A popular variation involves filling the buns with a creamy, sweet custard.
    *   **Red Bean Paste:** Sweet red bean paste is another traditional and delicious filling.
    *   **BBQ Pork (Cha Siu Bo Lo Bao):** For a savory twist, fill the buns with diced char siu (Chinese BBQ pork).
*   **Topping Variations:**
    *   **Chocolate:** Add a tablespoon of cocoa powder to the topping mixture for a chocolate pineapple bun.
    *   **Matcha:** Incorporate matcha powder for an earthy, green tea flavored topping.
    *   **Coconut:** Mix in a small amount of shredded coconut into the topping for added texture and flavor.
*   **Dough Substitutions:**
    *   **Dairy-Free:** Substitute dairy milk with plant-based milk (almond, soy, oat) and use a vegan butter substitute in both the dough and topping.
    *   **Whole Wheat:** While less traditional, you can substitute a portion of the white flour with whole wheat flour for a healthier twist, though the texture will be denser.
*   **Egg Wash Alternatives:** If you prefer a less shiny finish, you can brush the topping with milk or even just water before baking.

Baking your own Pineapple Buns is a rewarding experience that brings a taste of authentic Chinese bakery culture into your home. With this detailed guide and a little patience, you'll be able to create these delectable treats that are sure to impress. Embark on this culinary journey and discover the immense satisfaction of baking your very own, perfect Pineapple Bun Recipe at home. Enjoy the process, and savor every bite of your homemade Bo Lo Bao!
